mechanism_narrative: Sustained job growth, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates a healthy and expanding economy. This boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors price in future growth.
evidence_refs: fact:historical correlation of employment with economic growth
failure_condition: If PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth rate is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in retail sales indicates robust consumer spending, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and profits. This strong demand environment drives S&P 500 forward returns higher as market participants anticipate improved financial performance.
failure_condition: If, when RSAFS 3-month annualized growth rate exceeds 5%, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of such triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Low and stable inflation combined with a low federal funds rate creates an environment of low discount rates and strong purchasing power. This combination is highly supportive of equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as future cash flows are discounted at lower rates.
evidence_refs: fact:low rates and low inflation support equities
failure_condition: If, during periods where CPIAUCSL 12-month change is below 2.5% and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are consistently negative (e.g., average return < 0) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate, particularly from a low base, signals an impending economic slowdown or recession. This leads to decreased corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate a weaker economic environment.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.5 percentage points or more over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:unemployment as a lagging indicator of recession
failure_condition: If, during periods where UNRATE rises by 0.5pp over 3 months, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are predominantly positive (average return > 0) over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in housing starts reflects strong investment and consumer confidence in the future economy. This indicates a healthy demand environment that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns as construction activity drives broader economic growth.
evidence_refs: fact:housing as a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If the 3-month moving average of HOUST growth is positive, but the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of instances over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth indicates robust manufacturing and supply-side activity. This signals healthy corporate output and earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns as industrial firms report stronger performance.
failure_condition: If, when INDPRO 3-month annualized growth rate exceeds 3%, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of such instances over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: High employment combined with strong retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and purchasing power. This directly boosts corporate revenues and profits across various sectors, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns due to strong underlying economic activity.
evidence_refs: fact:strong labor market and consumer spending are synergistic
failure_condition: If, during periods of simultaneous positive PAYEMS and RSAFS 3-month growth, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 35% of observations over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained period of very low federal funds rates, especially after a crisis, indicates an accommodative monetary policy aimed at stimulating growth. This typically supports equity markets as discount rates remain low and liquidity is ample, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If, during periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 6 consecutive months, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are consistently negative or flat (average return <= 0) over any 3-year rolling window.
